matches.x: CHAPTER XI. David made king——He winneth the castle x Zion—David’s mighty men. . HEN. all Israel: gathered ‘them. selves. to David. unto Hebron, saying, Behold, we are thy bone and thy. flesh.
CHAPTER XI. David made king——He winneth the castle x Zion—David’s mighty men. . HEN. all Israel: gathered ‘them. selves. to David. unto Hebron, saying, Behold, we are thy bone and thy. flesh.

8 And David said on that day, Whosoever getteth up to the gutter, and smiteth the Jebusites, and the lame and the blind, that are hated of David’s. soul, he shall be chief and captain. Wherefore they said, The blind and the lame shall not come into the house.

:( And. David dwelt.in: the castle; therefore. they called. it. the. city of David... :

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Ibn Ezra on Isaiah 29:1:3
matches.x: where David hath dwelt; or the city, where David dwelt, since the whole sentence, with the past tense of a verb, can in this case be considered as a noun in the genitive governed by a preceding substantive; comp. בראשית ברא in the beginning, when God created, etc. (Gen. 1:1); תחלת דבר וגו׳ the beginning of the Lord’s speaking to Hosea (Hos. 1:1).
"It is generally agreed that there were now two tabernacles, one at Gibeon, and the other in the city of David, which one David had constructed as a temporary residence for the ark." (Clarke)

Shaul died, and Baal Hanan son of Akhbor reigned in his stead.
12 And of the’sons of ‘Azgad ; Toi hanan the son of Hakkatan, and with him a hundred and ten males.

WHO SMOTE MIDIAN IN THE FIELD OF MOAB. The intent thereof is to tell of Hadad’s prowess, for the Midianites had come into the field of Moab to overpower them, but he was victorious over them all.Baal-hanan the son of Achbor310Verse 38 here. was of the same place as Shaul of Rehoboth by the River,311Verse 37 here. in whose stead he reigned, and therefore Scripture does not ascribe another city to him. It is possible that “Hanan” is the name of a place, and he was the master thereof, which account
Shaul died, and Baal Hanan son of Akhbor reigned in his stead.

Obed ('serving') is the promised posterity that heals Naomi's barren state.
11 And the ark of the Lord continued. in the house of Obed-edom the Gittite three months; and the Lord blessed. Obed-edom, and all. his household.
